perturbation.original_text: Bravatek agrees to serve as a non-exclusive project sales lead finder for Fazync. In this capacity, Bravatek will use its best efforts to provide the following services to Fazync a. Promote, market and introduce the Products to prospective clients in the government space nationwide. b. Provide a quarterly Pipeline or project information leads report to Fazync a monthly basis which contains a 3-month rolling forecast of potential sales.
perturbation.changed_text: Bravatek agrees to serve as the exclusive project sales lead finder for Fazync. In this capacity, Bravatek shall provide the following services to Fazync a. Promote, market and introduce the Products to prospective clients in the government space nationwide. b. Provide a quarterly Pipeline or project information leads report to Fazync a monthly basis which contains a 3-month rolling forecast of potential sales.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' and 'will use its best efforts' to 'shall,' Bravatek's role is strengthened, creating an obligation to be the only sales lead finder. This directly conflicts with section 2a stating 'Listing Bravatek in all appropriate sales and marketing materials as a non-exclusive alliance partner' creating ambiguity on if the alliance is exclusive or non-exclusive with potential contradictory obligations on Fazync. The term 'non-exclusive' in Section 2 contradicts the term 'exclusive' in Section 1 now.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Fazync agrees to use its best efforts to promote and support project lead finding and after-sales support of Bravatek by: a. Listing Bravatek in all appropriate sales and marketing materials as a non-exclusive alliance partner (with focus of government customers) b. Provide timely responses to both technical and administrative questions posed by Bravatek. c. Promote Bravatek's product and service offerings whenever possible. d. Aid Bravatek in the writing of any technical/marketing/sales documents when requested and participate in mutually-agreed upon sales calls. e. Provide Bravatek with co-branded marketing material that can be emailed or handed to prospective clients.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' directly contradicts the original agreement where Bravatek was listed as a 'non-exclusive alliance partner.' This creates a clear and impactful conflict, making the contractual obligations regarding exclusivity ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The term 'non-exclusive' in Section 2 directly opposes the term 'exclusive' in Section 1, establishing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with Fazync via email to IHSI's CEO, Devon Jones, and delivered through Bravatek or a Fazync -designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by Fazync at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at Fazync bank account.
perturbation.changed_text: For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with Fazync via email to IHSI's CEO, Devon Jones, and delivered through Bravatek or a Fazync -designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee of 15% of project revenue, payable NET 60 days after the date of invoice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the lead-finder fee will be ‘mutually discussed and finally decided by Fazync at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20%’ where as the modified text fixes the fee at 15%. This creates an in-text contradiction as the original text gives Fazync the right to decide on the percentage, while the new text makes the fee fixed, removing the ‘mutually discussed’ and finally decided step. This also changes the NET payment days to 60 days, which now creates a fixed timeline for payment of Bravatek, instead of being dependent after each client payment on delivered products received at Fazync bank account.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When custom Products are designed, developed and to be delivered to Bravatek-identified perspective clients, the parties shall agree to a proposed sales price for use during the project in writing prior to the commencement of each project. For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with Fazync via email to IHSI's CEO, Devon Jones, and delivered through Bravatek or a Fazync -designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee of 15% of project revenue, payable NET 60 days after the date of invoice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text establishes a fixed lead-finder fee of 15% and a payment term of NET 60 days. This directly contradicts the original agreement where the fee was to be mutually discussed and fall within a range of 10-20%, and payment was contingent on client payments. The 'Contradicted Text' is identical to the 'Changed Text,' indicating the contradiction now resides within the same section, making it an unambiguous in-text contradiction. The modified section now conflicts with the clause about mutually agreed price for custom products.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may only be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice, if the other party is in material breach of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the condition that termination can only occur if there is a material breach, a conflict is introduced. The original agreement allowed either party to terminate with 90 days' notice, regardless of breach. The modified text restricts this right and creates a contradiction with section 7: 'The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term.' Now it is unclear, if termination can only occur during the original term of 12 months and the renewel period of another 12 months.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term. This Agreement may only be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice, if the other party is in material breach of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed termination with 90 days' notice regardless of breach, while the changed text restricts this right by requiring a material breach. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines the renewal process based on 90-day notice, creating uncertainty as to whether the agreement can be terminated outside of a material breach scenario as it suggests in the contradiction text.
